201.reason: [Error: String comparison using '==' instead of '.equals()' method. This will always compare object references, not the actual string content, causing all identifiers to be treated as IDENFR instead of keywords.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.07142857142857142 , reason:  [Error: String comparison using == instead of .equals() method. This will always be false for non-interned strings, causing "main" to be treated as an identifier instead of a keyword.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: String comparison using '==' instead of '.equals()' method. This will always compare object references, not the actual string content, causing all identifiers to be treated as IDENFR instead of keywords.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  Error: Uses == for string comparison; should use .equals() to compare string content
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.07142857142857142 , reason:  [Error: Using '==' to compare String objects checks for reference equality, not content equality. This causes keywords like 'main', 'int', 'printf' etc. to be incorrectly identified as IDENFR instead of their specific token types. Should use ident.equals("main")]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.07142857142857142 , reason:  [Error: Using '==' to compare String objects compares references, not content. This causes keywords like "main", "int", "printf" etc. to be incorrectly identified as IDENFR instead of their specific token types. Should use ident.equals("main")]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.07142857142857142 , reason:  [Using '==' to compare strings compares references, not content. Should use .equals()]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.07142857142857142 , reason:  [Using '==' to compare strings instead of .equals() leads to incorrect keyword recognition; this causes keywords like 'int' and 'printf' to be treated as IDENFR]
